event_name: Atomic Arch AUR Compromise and eBPF Rootkit
confidence_reason: Multiple sources confirm systematic hijacking of orphaned AUR packages to deliver the atomic-lockfile infostealer and deploy an eBPF rootkit for persistence.

payload_behavior: infostealing (credentials, cookies, SSH keys, wallet data)
optional eBPF rootkit deployment for process and file hiding

open_questions: The exact set of hijacked AUR packages during the June 9-12 window.
Full extent of credential abuse prior to C2 takedown.

defender_takeaways.detection: Monitor for unexpected pinned eBPF maps in /sys/fs/bpf and audit npm/bun cache directories for malicious dependencies.
defender_takeaways.hunting: Search systemd unit configurations for aggressive infinite restart settings (Restart=always, RestartSec=30) coupled with unknown binaries.
defender_takeaways.remediation: Rotate all cloud, developer, SSH, and session credentials that were present on the host; perform a complete OS reinstall if root access was obtained.
defender_takeaways.prevention: Review PKGBUILD and .install files from the AUR before running local builds, and restrict egress network connectivity from build environments.

containment_complete: Identify and remove compromised AUR packages
eradication_complete: Perform OS re-installation on infected hosts
recovery_complete: Restore configurations from known good backups
closure_required: Conduct system audit to verify no eBPF rootkit maps remain pinned
